---
title: "Session 1: Welcome, Recap & Tooling Upgrade"
date: "2026-03-12"
date-modified: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Date:** Thursday, 12 March 2026, 16:00–19:00
:::

## Learning goals

- Understand course structure, expectations, and assessment
- Refresh key R and Quarto skills from the prior course
- Set up a clean, reproducible project workflow (`.Rproj`, relative paths, folder structure)
- Upgrade Quarto skills: YAML front matter, multiple output formats, bibliography with BibTeX
- Understand the basics of Git version control via the RStudio Git pane

::: {.callout-important}
All questions should be posted on the dedicated [discussion page](https://github.com/data-science-ma/AdvancedDataScience26-Material/discussions).
This allows all students to benefit from responses and allows you to help each other. 
I do not answer questions via email.
:::

## Session flow

| Time | Activity |
|---|---|
| 16:00–16:20 | Welcome, course overview, assessment |
| 16:20–17:00 | Quarto upgrade walkthrough + live demo |
| 17:00–17:15 | Git orientation |
| 17:15–18:15 | Student exercise |
| 18:15–19:00 | Introduce Take-home Task 1, Q&A |

## Materials

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| [Slides](slides.qmd) | Lecture slides — open in browser, press `F` for fullscreen |
| [Demo document](demo.qmd) | Live coding document built during the session; use as a model for your own reports |
| [Exercise sheet](exercise.qmd) | In-session exercise — setting up a reproducible Quarto workflow |
| [References](references.bib) | BibTeX file with all citations used in session materials |

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Demo assignment to test GitHub Classroom
[Here](https://classroom.github.com/a/PRWNjriG) is the link to the demo assignment in Github classroom.

Remember: there is no submit button, your last commit before the deadline automatically counts as submission.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Downloading the source (.qmd) files
The demo document and exercise sheet have a **`</> Code`** button in the top-right corner. Click it and select **"View Source"** to see the raw `.qmd` file. Copy the full text, paste it into a new file in RStudio, and save it with a `.qmd` extension — you now have a fully working local copy.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Saving slides as PDF
**Option A (recommended):** Open the slides in Chrome or Edge, click ☰ (bottom-left) → Tools → PDF Export Mode, then **File → Print → Save as PDF**.

**Option B:** Add `?print-pdf` to the end of the URL, then **File → Print → Save as PDF**. Enable "Background graphics" in the print options for correct styling.
:::

## Recap exercises

- Browse the [previous course](https://researchmethodology-fall25.netlify.app/) and make sure you understand the content on linear regression (especially [session 10](https://researchmethodology-fall25.netlify.app/content/material/session10))
- After registering at GitHub, do the following two exercises of the [First day on GitHub](https://learn.github.com/skills) section:^[
For further readings see the [Github Quickstart Guide](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/start-your-journey))
]
  - Introduction to GitHub
  - Commmunicate using Markdown
- Check out the [discussion space](https://github.com/data-science-ma/AdvancedDataScience26-Material/discussions) on the GitHub material repository and write a short post (such as a self-introduction) into the `General` space

## Take-home task 1

Assigned at the end of this session.

- **Topic:** Regression basics — income and life expectancy across countries
- **Dataset:** World Bank Life Expectancy (182 countries, 2017) — [download from OSF](https://osf.io/pguwf/)
- **Due:** Thursday, 02 April 2026, 08:00
- **Submission:** GitHub Classroom (link distributed in session)
